projectCategory: JavaScript Arrays of Objects – 60 Practical Projects
subject: Web Development (JavaScript)

projects.title: Filter students with grade >= 80
projects.description: Given an array of student objects (each with name, grade), use `filter` to keep those with grade 80 or higher.
projects.exampleText: const highAchievers = students.filter(s => s.grade >= 80);
projects.learningOutcome: Using filter to select elements based on a condition.
projects.logicExplanation: `filter()` creates a new array with all elements that pass the test implemented by the provided function. It is used here to select students whose grade is above a threshold.
projects.codeExplanation: The code defines an array of student objects. The `filter` method keeps only those with `grade >= 80`.

projects.title: Map product names to uppercase
projects.description: Given an array of product objects (each with name, price), use `map` to create an array of uppercase product names.
projects.exampleText: const uppercaseNames = products.map(p => p.name.toUpperCase());
projects.learningOutcome: Using map to transform each element.
projects.logicExplanation: `map()` creates a new array populated with the results of calling a provided function on every element. It is used to transform the `name` property to uppercase.
projects.codeExplanation: Each product object's `name` is accessed and converted to uppercase, producing a new array of uppercase names.

projects.title: Reduce to calculate total price of products
projects.description: Given an array of product objects, use `reduce` to sum the prices.
projects.exampleText: const total = products.reduce((sum, p) => sum + p.price, 0);
projects.logicExplanation: `reduce()` executes a reducer function on each element, resulting in a single output value. It is perfect for summing numeric properties like price.
projects.codeExplanation: The reducer accumulates the total price by adding `product.price` to the accumulator, starting from 0.

projects.title: Find student by ID
projects.description: Given an array of student objects (each with id, name), use `find` to locate a student with a given ID.
projects.exampleText: const student = students.find(s => s.id === 101);
projects.learningOutcome: Using find to locate a specific object.
projects.logicExplanation: `find()` returns the first element that satisfies the provided testing function. It is used to locate a student object with a matching ID.
projects.codeExplanation: `find` searches the array and returns the student whose `id` equals the target.

projects.title: Find index of task by title
projects.description: Given an array of task objects (title, completed), use `findIndex` to find the index of a task with a specific title.
projects.exampleText: const index = tasks.findIndex(t => t.title === 'Buy milk');
projects.learningOutcome: Using findIndex to locate the position.
projects.logicExplanation: `findIndex()` returns the index of the first element that satisfies the testing function, or -1 if none. This helps locate where a task resides.
projects.codeExplanation: The function returns the index of the task with a given title; if not found, -1 is returned.

projects.title: Sort products by price ascending
projects.description: Given an array of product objects, use `sort` with a custom comparator to sort by price low to high.
projects.exampleText: const sorted = products.sort((a,b) => a.price - b.price);
projects.learningOutcome: Sorting objects by a numeric property.
projects.logicExplanation: `sort()` sorts the elements of an array in place. A custom comparator function is used to sort objects by their `price` property.
projects.codeExplanation: The comparator `(a, b) => a.price - b.price` sorts numbers in ascending order; descending is achieved by subtracting the other way.

projects.title: Check if any student failed (grade < 40)
projects.description: Given an array of student objects, use `some` to check if any has grade below 40.
projects.exampleText: const hasFailed = students.some(s => s.grade < 40);
projects.logicExplanation: `some()` tests whether at least one element in the array passes the test. It returns a boolean.
projects.codeExplanation: `some` checks if any student has `grade < 40` (failing grade).

projects.title: Check if all tasks are completed
projects.description: Given an array of task objects, use `every` to check if every task is marked completed.
projects.exampleText: const allDone = tasks.every(t => t.completed);
projects.learningOutcome: Using every to test all elements.
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Chain map and filter to get names of discounted products
projects.description: First filter products that have a discount > 0, then map to get their names.
projects.exampleText: const discountedNames = products.filter(p => p.discount > 0).map(p => p.name);
projects.learningOutcome: Chaining array methods for clean data processing.
projects.logicExplanation: Method chaining allows applying multiple array transformations in sequence. Here, `filter` first selects discounted products, then `map` extracts their names.
projects.codeExplanation: First `filter` keeps products with `discount > 0`, then `map` returns an array of `name` strings.

projects.title: Filter orders where customer city is 'New York'
projects.description: Given an array of order objects (each with customer: {city}), filter orders from New York.
projects.exampleText: const nyOrders = orders.filter(o => o.customer.city === 'New York');
projects.learningOutcome: Filtering based on nested object properties.
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Map to extract full names from students
projects.description: Given an array of student objects (firstName, lastName), map to create an array of full names.
projects.exampleText: const fullNames = students.map(s => `${s.firstName} ${s.lastName}`);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Filter products with low stock (<5) and high price (>100)
projects.description: Filter products where stock is less than 5 and price greater than 100.
projects.exampleText: const critical = products.filter(p => p.stock < 5 && p.price > 100);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Reduce to group products by category
projects.description: Given an array of products (category, name, price), use reduce to create an object grouping products by category.
projects.exampleText: const grouped = products.reduce((acc, p) => { acc[p.category] = [...(acc[p.category] || []), p]; return acc; }, {});
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Find the most expensive product
projects.description: Use reduce to find the product with the highest price.
projects.exampleText: const mostExpensive = products.reduce((max, p) => p.price > max.price ? p : max);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Sort students by total marks descending
projects.description: Given an array of student objects (name, totalMarks), sort them from highest marks to lowest.
projects.exampleText: const sorted = students.sort((a,b) => b.totalMarks - a.totalMarks);
projects.logicExplanation: Sorting by a numeric property (like total marks) uses a comparator that subtracts one property from another.
projects.codeExplanation: `(a, b) => b.totalMarks - a.totalMarks` sorts in descending order.

projects.title: Check if any product has rating > 4.5
projects.description: Use `some` to check if any product has a rating greater than 4.5.
projects.exampleText: const hasHighRated = products.some(p => p.rating > 4.5);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Check if all books are in stock
projects.description: Use `every` to verify that every book has `inStock === true`.
projects.exampleText: const allAvailable = books.every(b => b.inStock);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Chain: get discounted products, then filter cheap ones (<50)
projects.description: First filter products with discount, then filter those with price < 50, then map to names.
projects.exampleText: const cheapDiscountedNames = products.filter(p => p.discount > 0).filter(p => p.price < 50).map(p => p.name);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Deep nested: filter posts with at least one positive comment
projects.description: Given an array of posts (title, comments: [{text, sentiment}]), keep posts that have at least one comment with sentiment 'positive'.
projects.exampleText: const positivePosts = posts.filter(post => post.comments.some(c => c.sentiment === 'positive'));
projects.learningOutcome: Filtering based on nested array condition.
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Map and filter to get active user emails
projects.description: First filter active users, then map to get their emails.
projects.exampleText: const activeEmails = users.filter(u => u.active).map(u => u.email);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Reduce to calculate average age of users
projects.description: Use reduce to sum ages, then divide by the number of users.
projects.exampleText: const avgAge = users.reduce((sum, u) => sum + u.age, 0) / users.length;
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Find user by email
projects.description: Use `find` to locate a user object with a given email.
projects.exampleText: const user = users.find(u => u.email === 'jane@example.com');
projects.learningOutcome: Locating an object by a unique property.
projects.logicExplanation: `find` returns the user object whose email matches.
projects.codeExplanation: `users.find(user => user.email === targetEmail)`

projects.title: Find index of product with specific SKU
projects.description: Use `findIndex` to find the index of a product by its SKU string.
projects.exampleText: const idx = products.findIndex(p => p.sku === 'XYZ123');
projects.learningOutcome: Finding index based on a property.
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Sort tasks by priority (high to low) and due date (earliest first)
projects.description: Tasks have priority (1=high, 2=medium, 3=low) and dueDate (ISO string). Sort by priority first, then by due date.
projects.exampleText: const sorted = tasks.sort((a,b) => a.priority - b.priority || new Date(a.dueDate) - new Date(b.dueDate));
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Some and every: validate required fields in user objects
projects.description: Given an array of user objects, use `every` to check that all have required fields (name, email).
projects.exampleText: const allValid = users.every(u => u.name && u.email);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Reduce to count occurrences of each status
projects.description: Given an array of tasks with a status property, use reduce to count how many tasks have each status.
projects.exampleText: const statusCount = tasks.reduce((acc, t) => { acc[t.status] = (acc[t.status] || 0) + 1; return acc; }, {});
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Map to transform objects to a different shape (e.g., API response)
projects.description: Given an array of students with `firstName`, `lastName`, `age`, map to a new array of objects `{ id, fullName, age }` where id is generated.
projects.exampleText: const transformed = students.map((s, i) => ({ id: i+1, fullName: `${s.firstName} ${s.lastName}`, age: s.age }));
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Filter products with at least one review > 4 stars
projects.description: Each product has a `reviews` array (each review has `rating`). Filter products that have any review with rating > 4.
projects.exampleText: const highlyRated = products.filter(p => p.reviews.some(r => r.rating > 4));
projects.learningOutcome: Filtering based on nested array condition.
projects.logicExplanation: Use `some` on the reviews array inside the filter condition.
projects.codeExplanation: `products.filter(p => p.reviews.some(r => r.rating > 4))`

projects.title: Chain: get top 3 highest rated products
projects.description: Sort products by rating descending, then take the first three.
projects.exampleText: const top3 = products.sort((a,b) => b.rating - a.rating).slice(0,3);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Reduce to compute statistics (min, max, sum) for scores
projects.description: Given an array of scores (or objects with score), use reduce to compute min, max, sum, and count.
projects.exampleText: const stats = scores.reduce((acc, s) => ({ min: Math.min(acc.min, s), max: Math.max(acc.max, s), sum: acc.sum + s, count: acc.count + 1 }), { min: Infinity, max: -Infinity, sum: 0, count: 0 });
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Sort with custom comparator for multiple criteria (name then age)
projects.description: Given an array of people (name, age), sort first by name alphabetically, then by age (younger first) for same name.
projects.exampleText: const sorted = people.sort((a,b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name) || a.age - b.age);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Find employee with highest salary
projects.description: Use reduce to find the employee object with maximum salary.
projects.exampleText: const topEarner = employees.reduce((max, e) => e.salary > max.salary ? e : max);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Check if any order is pending
projects.description: Use `some` to see if any order has status 'pending'.
projects.exampleText: const hasPending = orders.some(o => o.status === 'pending');
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Check if all items in cart are in stock
projects.description: Use `every` to verify that each item in a cart has `inStock === true`.
projects.exampleText: const allAvailable = cart.items.every(i => i.inStock);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Map and reduce to get total quantity of items in cart
projects.description: First map to extract quantity from each item, then reduce to sum.
projects.exampleText: const totalQty = cart.items.map(i => i.quantity).reduce((sum, q) => sum + q, 0);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Filter and map to get discounted prices for eligible products
projects.description: Filter products that are on sale, then map to compute discounted price.
projects.exampleText: const salePrices = products.filter(p => p.onSale).map(p => p.price * (1 - p.discount));
projects.logicExplanation: Filter products on sale, then map to calculate discounted price.
projects.codeExplanation: `products.filter(p => p.onSale).map(p => p.price * (1 - p.discount))`

projects.title: Reduce to build a lookup object by ID
projects.description: Given an array of objects with unique IDs, use reduce to create an object mapping ID to the object.
projects.exampleText: const lookup = items.reduce((map, item) => { map[item.id] = item; return map; }, {});
projects.learningOutcome: Creating a lookup map with reduce.
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Find and update object in array
projects.description: Find a task by ID, then mark it as completed.
projects.exampleText: const task = tasks.find(t => t.id === 5); if (task) task.completed = true;
projects.exampleOutput: The task object modified in place
projects.logicExplanation: Use `find` to locate the object, then modify its property directly.
projects.codeExplanation: `const task = tasks.find(t => t.id === id); if (task) task.completed = true;`

projects.title: Sort array of objects by date
projects.description: Given an array of events (date string 'YYYY-MM-DD'), sort by date ascending.
projects.exampleText: const sorted = events.sort((a,b) => new Date(a.date) - new Date(b.date));
projects.logicExplanation: Sort by date by converting strings to Date objects or comparing timestamps.
projects.codeExplanation: `events.sort((a,b) => new Date(a.date) - new Date(b.date))`

projects.title: Some and every combination: check if any product out of stock and all are discontinued
projects.description: Check if there is any product with stock 0 AND all products are discontinued.
projects.exampleText: const result = products.some(p => p.stock === 0) && products.every(p => p.discontinued);
projects.logicExplanation: Check if any product is out of stock AND all are discontinued? (complex condition)
projects.codeExplanation: `products.some(p => p.stock === 0) && products.every(p => p.discontinued)`

projects.title: Deep map: transform nested objects (add full name to each student in courses)
projects.description: Given courses, each with an array of students (firstName, lastName), map to add fullName to each student.
projects.exampleText: const updated = courses.map(c => ({ ...c, students: c.students.map(s => ({ ...s, fullName: `${s.firstName} ${s.lastName}` })) }));
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Filter based on conditions involving multiple object properties
projects.description: Filter products that are either in category 'electronics' and price < 500, or in category 'clothing' and onSale.
projects.exampleText: const selected = products.filter(p => (p.category === 'electronics' && p.price < 500) || (p.category === 'clothing' && p.onSale));
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Reduce to group and count by category
projects.description: Group items by category and count how many per category.
projects.exampleText: const counts = items.reduce((acc, i) => { acc[i.category] = (acc[i.category] || 0) + 1; return acc; }, {});
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Find the first product meeting complex condition
projects.description: Find the first product that is in stock, price < 100, and has rating > 4.
projects.exampleText: const product = products.find(p => p.stock > 0 && p.price < 100 && p.rating > 4);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Sort and limit results
projects.description: Sort products by price ascending, then take the cheapest 5.
projects.exampleText: const cheapest5 = products.sort((a,b) => a.price - b.price).slice(0,5);
projects.logicExplanation: `sort` then `slice` to get the top N items.
projects.codeExplanation: `products.sort((a,b) => b.rating - a.rating).slice(0,5)`

projects.title: Chaining: map to add tax, filter high tax, reduce to total tax
projects.description: Given an array of items (price), map to compute tax (10%), filter those with tax > 20, then reduce to sum tax.
projects.exampleText: const totalTax = items.map(i => i.price * 0.1).filter(tax => tax > 20).reduce((sum, t) => sum + t, 0);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Nested reduce: total cost of all items in all orders
projects.description: Given orders (each with items: {price, quantity}), calculate total cost across all orders.
projects.exampleText: const total = orders.reduce((sum, order) => sum + order.items.reduce((sub, item) => sub + item.price * item.qty, 0), 0);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Map to create HTML list items
projects.description: Given an array of product names, map to generate <li> elements as strings.
projects.exampleText:
const listHTML = products.map(p => `
  • ${p.name}
    • `).join('');
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Filter users by age range then map to names
projects.description: Filter users between 18 and 30, then map to extract their names.
projects.exampleText: const youngNames = users.filter(u => u.age >= 18 && u.age <= 30).map(u => u.name);
projects.logicExplanation: `filter` to get users within age range, then `map` to extract names.
projects.codeExplanation: `users.filter(u => u.age >= 18 && u.age <= 30).map(u => u.name)`

projects.title: Reduce to find the object with max value (reusable)
projects.description: Write a generic reduce function to find max by a given key.
projects.exampleText: const maxProduct = products.reduce((max, p) => p.price > max.price ? p : max);
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Some and every with array of conditions
projects.description: Create an array of predicate functions and test if all (or any) pass for a given object.
projects.exampleText: const conditions = [p => p.stock > 0, p => p.price < 100]; const ok = conditions.every(cond => cond(product));
projects.logicExplanation: You can create an array of predicates and use `every` or `some` to test all or any.
projects.codeExplanation: `conditions.every(cond => cond(object))`

projects.title: Sort with dynamic comparator
projects.description: Write a function that returns a comparator based on a property name and sort order.
projects.exampleText: const sortBy = (prop, asc = true) => (a,b) => asc ? a[prop] - b[prop] : b[prop] - a[prop];
projects.logicExplanation: A function that returns a comparator based on a property name and order.
projects.codeExplanation: `function dynamicSort(prop, asc = true) { return (a,b) => asc ? a[prop] - b[prop] : b[prop] - a[prop]; }`

projects.title: Find index of object and remove it (splice)
projects.description: Find the index of a product by SKU, then remove it from the array using splice.
projects.exampleText: const idx = products.findIndex(p => p.sku === 'XYZ'); if (idx !== -1) products.splice(idx, 1);
projects.exampleOutput: Products array with that element removed
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Chain multiple methods to get statistics summary
projects.description: Given a list of scores, use filter, map, reduce to compute average of scores above a threshold.
projects.exampleText: const avgHigh = scores.filter(s => s > 70).reduce((sum, s, _, arr) => sum + s / arr.length, 0);
projects.logicExplanation: Chain filter, map, reduce to produce a summary object.
projects.codeExplanation: `users.filter(u => u.active).map(u => u.score).reduce((stats, score) => ({ sum: stats.sum + score, count: stats.count + 1 }), { sum: 0, count: 0 })`

projects.title: Deep filter: find users who have purchased a specific product
projects.description: Given users with orders (each with items array), find users who have purchased a product named 'Laptop'.
projects.exampleText: const laptopBuyers = users.filter(user => user.orders.some(order => order.items.some(item => item.name === 'Laptop')));
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Reduce to create a frequency map of tags
projects.description: Given an array of articles (each with tags array), reduce to count occurrences of each tag.
projects.exampleText: const tagCount = articles.flatMap(a => a.tags).reduce((freq, tag) => { freq[tag] = (freq[tag] || 0) + 1; return freq; }, {});
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Map and filter to get active users with specific role
projects.description: Filter active users with role 'admin', then map to names.
projects.exampleText: const adminNames = users.filter(u => u.active && u.role === 'admin').map(u => u.name);
projects.logicExplanation: Filter by active and role, then map to names.
projects.codeExplanation: `users.filter(u => u.active && u.role === 'admin').map(u => u.name)`

projects.title: Sort by calculated property (e.g., discount amount)
projects.description: Sort products by the amount of discount (price * discount) descending.
projects.exampleText: const sorted = products.sort((a,b) => (b.price * b.discount) - (a.price * a.discount));
projects.logicExplanation: Compute a derived property (e.g., discount amount) and sort by it.
projects.codeExplanation: `products.sort((a,b) => (a.price * a.discount) - (b.price * b.discount))`

projects.title: Chaining with reduce to transform and aggregate
projects.description: Use reduce to both filter and transform in one pass: keep only items with price > 50 and collect their names.
projects.exampleText: const expensiveNames = products.reduce((acc, p) => p.price > 50 ? [...acc, p.name] : acc, []);
projects.learningOutcome: Filter and map in one reduce.
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.

projects.title: Comprehensive practice: combine all methods to solve a real‑world problem
projects.description: Given an array of orders, compute the total revenue for each product category, then sort categories by revenue descending, and output the top 3.
projects.exampleText: A pipeline using flatMap, reduce, sort, slice, etc.
projects.exampleOutput: Array of category objects with revenue
projects.logicExplanation: This project practices array methods on objects to solve a data manipulation problem.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates the use of appropriate array methods to achieve the task.
